title: Ephemerantha poilanei
intro: Ephemerantha poilanei, also known as the Vietnamese orchid tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Ephemerantha poilanei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
